producer.name: Klein Constantia
producer.philosophy: One of the oldest wine farms in the New World, established in 1685 by Simon van der Stel, the first Governor of the Cape. The original Constantia estate was famed for its Vin de Constance — a sweet Muscat wine beloved by Napoleon Bonaparte during his exile on St. Helena. The wine was revived in 1986. Klein Constantia's vineyards are cooled by ocean breezes from False Bay and the Atlantic, creating one of the coolest wine-growing climates in South Africa.

metadata.soil: Decomposed granite and Table Mountain sandstone — ancient weathered soils, excellent drainage
metadata.historical_note: Original Constantia estate divided in 1712. Klein Constantia is the smaller portion. Vin de Constance was the most expensive wine in the world in the 18th-19th centuries.

wines.name: Vin de Constance
wines.style: Historic natural sweet wine — Napoleon's favorite
wines.vinification_notes: 100% Muscat de Frontignan (Muscat Blanc à Petits Grains). Late-harvested in multiple passes through the vineyard over 2-3 months, picking only botrytized and raisined berries. Fermented in French oak, aged 3-5 years in barrel. No fortification — naturally sweet from concentrated grape sugars. The original Vin de Constance was described by Jane Austen, Charles Dickens, and Charles Baudelaire.

wines.name: Sauvignon Blanc
wines.vinification_notes: 100% Sauvignon Blanc from cool, ocean-influenced vineyards. Tank fermented with some wild yeast. Crisp, mineral, and herbaceous — a style more Loire Valley than Marlborough. The cool Constantia climate preserves natural acidity.

wines.name: Chardonnay
wines.vinification_notes: 100% Chardonnay from high-elevation vineyards (up to 350m). Barrel fermented in French oak (30% new). Partial malolactic. Citrus, white peach, and subtle oak spice with a mineral backbone from the granite soils.

wines.name: Riesling
wines.style: Dry Riesling — rare in South Africa
wines.vinification_notes: 100% Riesling — one of the few South African producers with Riesling. Tank fermented, no oak, no malolactic. The cool Constantia climate (one of the coolest in SA) allows Riesling to develop properly. Lime, white flower, and petrol with age.

wines.name: Cabernet Sauvignon/Merlot
wines.style: Bordeaux blend from cool maritime terroir
wines.vinification_notes: Cabernet Sauvignon dominant with Merlot and Cabernet Franc. Aged in French oak (40% new). The cool Constantia climate produces a more restrained, elegant style of red than warmer SA regions — more Bordeaux than Stellenbosch.
